message: SwiGLU + depth 10. Build on SwiGLU win with more capacity.
SwiGLU + depth 10 + batch 2^18 + 5% warmup. Combo of promising directions.
Halve batch size to 2**18 (~262K tokens). More optimizer steps in fixed time budget may help convergence.
SwiGLU + half batch size (2^18). More steps per budget.
SwiGLU + higher matrix LR 0.06. Test if SwiGLU benefits from stronger optimization.

message: Higher learning rates: matrix_lr=0.06 (from 0.04), embedding_lr=0.8 (from 0.6). Test if baseline LRs are too conservative.
Warmup base + SwiGLU. Combining two proven improvements.
Warmup 10% (from 5%). Test if more warmup helps further.
Warmup base + batch 2^18. More optimization steps.
Use all-long attention (window_pattern='L'). No sliding window restriction - full context for every layer. Tests if sliding window is hurting quality.
Warmup base + matrix LR 0.06. Warmup should allow higher LR.

message: Longer warmdown: 70% instead of 50%. More gradual LR decay may help final convergence.
SwiGLU + warmdown 70% (from 50%). Longer cooldown for SwiGLU.
SwiGLU + raise logit softcap from 15 to 30. Less aggressive capping may help.
SwiGLU + head dim 64 (from 128). More heads, better capacity use.
Warmup base + weight decay 0.1 (from 0.2). Less regularization.

message: Lower weight decay from 0.2 to 0.1. With short training, less regularization may help.
Batch 2^18 + 5% warmup. Both showed improvements independently.
Batch 2^18 + higher LRs (matrix=0.06, embed=0.8). More steps = can push LR.
Batch 2^18 + 5% warmup + matrix LR 0.06. Triple combo.
Batch 2^18 + warmdown 70%. Longer cooldown with more steps.
Batch 2^18 + weight decay 0.1. Less regularization with more steps.
Batch 2^17 + device_batch 64 (fix assertion). More optimizer steps.
Final LR frac 0.1 (from 0.0). Don't decay LR fully to zero.
Aspect ratio 96 (from 64). Wider model: 768d -> ~1024d (8 heads). Test width vs compute.
Aspect ratio 96 (from 64). Wider model: 768d -> ~1024d (8 heads). Test width vs compute.
Wide model + warmdown 70%. Warmdown helped with smaller batch.
Wide model + WD 0.05 (from 0.1). Lower WD helped before.
Wide model + batch 2^17. More steps for bigger model.
Shallow-wide: depth 6 + aspect 128. Wide but fewer layers = faster steps.
Batch 2^16 on wide model. Continue batch reduction.
Head dim 64 (from 128) on wide master. More but smaller heads.
Aspect 128 + device_batch 32 (avoid OOM). Even wider model.
Warmdown 70% + final LR 0.1. Two schedule improvements combined.
Warmdown 70% + WD 0.05. Both helped individually, try combined.
WD 0.05 + final LR 0.1. Combine two near-misses.
Batch 2^18 on wide model. Test if wider model prefers larger batch.
Remove logit softcap. Simplify model - softcap may not help.
SSSSL window pattern. More short windows = more efficient attention.
All-long window pattern (L). No sliding window restriction.
All-long window pattern (L). No sliding window restriction.
Aspect 80 (from 96). Slightly narrower = faster steps = more data seen.
Disable value embeddings. Save params/compute for more training steps.
Remove x0 residual (simplify). Test if x0 skip connection is needed.
Cosine LR warmdown (instead of linear). Smoother decay trajectory.
Embedding LR 0.8 (from 0.6). Higher embedding learning rate.
Muon ns_steps 3 (from 5). Faster optimizer steps = more training.
GQA: KV heads = num_heads // 2. Less KV params, more efficient attention.
Unembedding LR 0.008 (from 0.004). Higher output head LR.
Batch 2^16 on grad clip base. Even more steps.
3% warmup on grad clip base. Small warmup helped before.
Scalar LR 1.0 (from 0.5). Higher LR for per-layer scales.
Depth 10 + aspect 80. Deeper but narrower to keep params similar.
Remove softcap on grad clip base. Grad clip may replace softcap.
Aspect 112 + device_batch 32. Wider model properly sized.
Matrix LR 0.045 + softcap 20. Both near-misses combined.
All LRs bumped: matrix 0.045, embed 0.8, unembed 0.006.
Softcap 18 + matrix LR 0.045. Combine two improvements.
Softcap 18 + matrix LR 0.045 + embed LR 0.8. Triple combo.
WD 0.1 (with existing decay schedule). Higher starting WD decays to 0.
Adam beta2 0.99 (from 0.95). Longer second moment memory.
SSL window pattern (from SSSL). More efficient attention.
SwiGLU on grad clip + wide + batch17 base. Revisit activation.
WD 0.15 (from 0.1). Push WD higher with decay schedule.
WD 0.2 (original value). With grad clip + improved schedule.
Softcap 18 + matrix LR 0.045 on WD 0.1 base.
Cosine warmdown curve (same 70% ratio). Smoother LR decay.
Simple residual: x = x + 0.1*x0 (remove learned lambdas).
Quadratic WD decay: WD*(1-progress)^2. Faster early decay.
VE gate channels 64 (from 32). More expressive value gating.
Embed init std 0.5 on MLR 0.042 base.
VE gate channels 64 (from 32). More expressive value embedding gating.
VE gate 128. Full head dim channels.
Batch 2^18 (from 2^17). Larger batch for wider model.
SSSSL window pattern. More short windows.
VE gate init with std=0.1 (from zeros).
Quarter-context short windows (from half). Faster attention.
Shorter sliding window (512 vs 1024). Cheaper attention in short-window layers enables more training steps.
x0 init 0.05 + muon warmup 200. Combine two near-misses.
Shorter sliding window (512) + x0_lambda init 0.05. Both near-misses, combined.
1/8 context short window. Even cheaper attention.
1/16 context window. Push window reduction further.
All-S pattern on 1/8 window. Maximum savings.
Short window 512 + x0_lambda 0.05 + matrix_lr 0.044. Triple combo of near-misses.
x0_lambda init 0.05 + matrix_lr 0.044. Both improve on short-window master.
Depth 10 + aspect 80. More depth with fast attention.
Batch 2^16 + db 32. More steps with fast attention.
GC every 10000 steps (from 5000). Less GC overhead.
Unembedding LR 0.006 (from 0.004). Higher lm_head learning rate improves output layer training.
Unembedding LR 0.006 (from 0.004). Higher lm_head LR accelerates output layer convergence.
Unembedding LR 0.006 (from 0.004). Higher lm_head LR for faster output convergence.
Final LR fraction 0.05 (from 0.0). Non-zero final LR keeps learning at end of warmdown.
Final LR frac 0.03 (from 0.05). Slightly lower final LR is better.
Final LR frac 0.02 (from 0.03). Fine-tuning final LR.
Resid lambda LR doubled (0.01 to 0.02). Faster residual scale learning.
VE gate channels 96 (from 64). More expressive value gating in sweet spot between 64 and 128.
VE gate 96 + x0 LR tripled (3x from 2x). More expressive gating + faster x0 learning.
RLR mult 0.04 (from 0.03). Continuing trend of resid_lambda LR increases (0.01->0.02->0.03 each improved). val_bpb 0.969488 vs master 0.969756.
Grad clip 2.0 (from 1.0). Higher clip allows larger gradient steps for scalar params (x0_lambdas with LR=2.8). Local val_bpb 0.969301 vs master 0.969664.
Grad clip 2.0 (from 1.0). Larger clip allows full gradient steps for high-LR scalar params (x0_lambdas LR=2.8). Local val_bpb 0.969454 vs master 0.969558.
Remove grad clip. Muon normalizes matrix gradients internally (X/X.norm), making global clipping redundant. Local val_bpb 0.969072 vs master 0.969558.

WARMDOWN_RATIO 0.75→0.70. More time at full LR (30%=90s vs 25%=75s) before convergence. Tested 0.60/0.65/0.70: all better than master, 0.70 best. Local val_bpb 0.969112 vs master 0.969558 (Δ=0.000446).

WDR 0.80→0.90. Continuing the WD80% trend. Master progressed 0.75→0.80, now trying 0.90 (only 10% at full LR). Local bpb 0.969330 vs new master local 0.969620 (Δ=0.000290). WDR=0.85 also tested (0.969578), 0.90 is best.

Muon beta2 0.95→0.9. More responsive variance tracking in NorMuon (faster adaptation of step sizes). Local val_bpb 0.968912 vs new master local 0.969620 (Δ=0.000708, largest improvement seen). Also tested beta2=0.99 (worse, 0.970005).

Inductor optimizations: coordinate_descent_tuning, epilogue_fusion, aggressive_fusion, shape_padding, max_autotune_pointwise + CUDA event timing + cudagraph_mark_step_begin. Pure throughput improvement, no model changes.
Inductor opts + Muon buffer pre-alloc + CUDA event timing + cudagraph_mark_step_begin. Eliminates torch.stack allocation overhead in Muon optimizer.
WEIGHT_DECAY=0.15 (from 0.125). Marginal local improvement, may help on server.
WD=0.15 + attn_scale=1.2 + muon_warmup=200. Three marginal improvements combined.
Linear WD decay (from quadratic). WD stays higher longer during training. New local best.
WD=0.13 + FINAL_LR_FRAC=0.02. Two marginal improvements combined.
WD=0.13 + FINAL_LR_FRAC=0.015. Tighter final LR + slightly higher WD.
WD=0.13 + FLR=0.02 + WARMDOWN=0.83. Tuning our best combo further.
FLR=0.02 + WARMDOWN=0.83. FLR=0.02 was our best server result.
FLR=0.02 + WARMDOWN=0.84. Fine-tuning schedule around best FLR.
FLR=0.02 + MLR=0.044. Slightly higher Muon LR with lower final LR.
FLR=0.02 + ELR=0.72. Combining best FLR with slightly lower embed LR.
FLR=0.0 (from 0.025). Decay LR completely to zero.
FLR=0.02 + resid_lambda_init=0.95 (from 1.0). Softer residual at init.
FLR=0.02 + lm_head_WD=0.003 (from 0.002). Slightly more unembedding regularization.
FLR=0.02 + Adam beta2=0.96 (from 0.95). More stable second moment.
FLR=0.02+WDR=0.835. Fine-tuning warmdown around best FLR.
FLR=0.02+WDR=0.84. Fine-tuning warmdown around best FLR.
FLR=0.02+WDR=0.82. Fine-tuning warmdown around best FLR.
FLR=0.021 rep4. Closest to master at 0.962779.
FLR=0.021 rep5. Closest to master at 0.962779.

FLR=0.021 rep8. Closest to master at 0.962779.
VE gate scale 2.0 (from 1.8). Stronger value residual.
VE gate scale 1.5 (from 1.8). Weaker value residual.
QK norm scaling 1.15. Amplify normalized Q,K before RoPE.
FLR=0.021 rep9. Pure FLR change, our closest to master.
FLR=0.021 rep10. Pure FLR change, our closest to master.
FLR=0.021 rep11. Pure FLR change, our closest to master.
FLR=0.021 rep12. Pure FLR change, our closest to master.
FLR=0.021 rep13. Pure FLR change, our closest to master.
Adam beta1=0.85 (from 0.8). More momentum for Adam params.
QK norm after RoPE (swap order). Norm on rotated features.
VE gate channels 64 (from 128). Smaller gating input.
Attn c_proj small init (uniform -0.001,0.001) instead of zeros.
VE layers 0,6,7 (drop layer 3) + FLR=0.021. Fewer VE = faster + quality LR.
wte_std=0.5 + FLR=0.021. Smaller embed init for faster convergence.
FLR=0.021 + WARMDOWN=1.0. Full decay from start.
WD decay exponent 1.5 (from 2). Between linear and quadratic.
FLR=0.021 + WD exp 1.5. Combo: better final LR + less aggressive WD decay.
FLR=0.021 + Muon mom target=0.93. Higher momentum.
Grad accum for embed+lm_head: update every 2 steps (fixed).
Batch schedule: 2^16 first 50%, then 2^17. More steps early, stable later.
Untie embed/lm_head at 2/3 training. Share early, specialize late.
Window warmup: short window ramps 64->256 over 200 steps. Cheaper attn early.
Bigram hash embedding (XOR adjacent tokens). Local context via embeddings.
FLR=0.021 rep14. Shotgunning for lucky variance.
Sparse attention gate: per-dim sigmoid gate (1,n_embd) on attn output.

Per-layer LR: deeper layers get 3% lower LR each. Layer 0=1.0, layer 7=0.79.
NorMuon re-enable: beta2=0.5 (from 0.0). Moderate variance tracking.
Learned softcap via log-space param (init=15, clamped 5-30, lr=0.01).
Partitioned hyperconnections: split residual into 2 halves with independent lambdas.

Muon momentum warmup 50 steps (from 100). Faster warmup.
Asymmetric logit rescale: positive*1.1, negative*0.9 before softcap.
Paired heads: odd heads use negated query for attention diversity.
